layman_headline: Fear grips the crypto market — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful state with Bitcoin sliding below $64K after rising US bond yields spooked investors. Most altcoins are bleeding, and Bitcoin dominance at 56.6% means money is flowing away from smaller coins. While Ethereum shows some early bullish chart signals like a 'Three White Soldiers' candlestick pattern, the broader environment is too hostile to commit capital with high conviction right now.
layman_action: Wait on the sidelines. If Ethereum pulls back to the $1,800-$1,820 range and holds that support while the Fear & Greed Index stabilizes or improves, that would be a stronger entry point. Don't chase in a fearful market without clear confirmation.
expert_reasoning: Evaluating the swing candidates: DEXE dropped 88.8% in 7 days — extreme volatility with data inconsistencies (current price listed as $3.87 but chart says $6.18, violating Rule 8's consistency check). BONK has the strongest RSI signal at 29.14 (oversold) but bearish chart bias and meme-coin risk in a risk-off environment make it unsuitable. STX is near oversold at 34.89 RSI but bearish structure with -96.2% from ATH signals structural decline. ETH stands out with a Three White Soldiers pattern, above SMA20, R/R of 2.55x, and $41B TVL stability. However, with Fear & Greed at 28, BTC dominance at 56.6% crushing alts, and the macro headline of rising bond yields/rate-hike odds, entering now lacks sufficient confirmation. Confidence capped at 30% given limited signal availability (no funding rates, no MACD crosses, no volume data across all candidates — per Rule 7).
risk_factors: Rising US bond yields and renewed Fed rate-hike expectations could trigger further crypto selloffs
BTC dominance at 56.6% actively suppresses altcoin performance — ETH could underperform even if BTC stabilizes
Ledger private key vulnerability news (CryptoSlate) could damage broader crypto confidence and trigger security-related selling
watch_list: BTC price action around $64K support — a break below $62K would invalidate any altcoin long setups
Fear & Greed Index trend — currently 28 and labeled 'improving'; needs to cross above 35 for safer entries
ETH price behavior at $1,900 support and $2,000 resistance — a close above $2,000 confirms the Three White Soldiers pattern
market_context: The crypto market is in a 'Fear' state (28/100) with BTC falling below $64K on macro headwinds from surging bond yields. Bitcoin dominance at 56.6% signals capital rotation away from altcoins, making this a hostile environment for new altcoin swing trades.
context_banner: Macro headwinds from rising bond yields dominate — disciplined patience beats forced entries in a fearful, alt-unfriendly market.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) with week average of 28.4 — persistent fear environment historically unfavorable for new long entries, though trend labeled 'improving' offers a glimmer of hope.
Bitcoin fell under $64K as surging US bond yields boosted Fed rate-hike odds — this macro headwind is the primary driver of current market weakness.
ETH RSI at 43.95 is neutral but combined with a Three White Soldiers candlestick pattern suggests early bullish momentum building; BONK RSI at 29.14 is oversold but in a bearish structure.
Ledger 7-year bug allowing private key reconstruction from 5 signatures is a security shock that could weigh on crypto sentiment broadly.

ETH shows the best chart setup among candidates with a slightly bullish bias (+0.30), Three White Soldiers pattern, and 2.55x R/R ratio — but below-all-MAs structure on most alts confirms bearish broader trend.
